description: String rendered on the web home page. Flip via the admin API to prove the feature-flag pipeline (Sprint 0 acceptance criterion).
Whether the home page shows the live API health/version line. Demonstrates a typed (bool) feature flag.
Days an unverified account keeps full use (incl. rating) after signup (ADR-011 soft verification). Past this, rating requires a verified email.
Minimum days between handle changes (Sprint 2 spec).
Ratings in a category before its profile counts as 'warming'. Below this the feed leans on popularity and shows no match percentages.
Ratings before full profile confidence (Sprint 3 spec's min-5, as a tier). Drives the 'needs N more ratings' prompt and feed shape.
Nightly full palate-profile recompute (heals drift after catalog vector changes; per-rating recompute covers the rest).
UTC hour for the nightly palate recompute (9 = ~3am US Central).
Nearest-neighbor candidate pool per category for the sectioned feed.
Pool rank where Up Your Alley ends and Stretch a Little begins.
Pool rank where Stretch a Little ends and the Wildcard sample space begins.
Items per feed section at warm/full confidence.
Items per Alley/Stretch section for cold users (confidence-adaptive shape).
Wildcard picks for full-confidence users.
Wildcard picks for cold/warm users — exploration teaches the engine fastest early.
Weight (percent) of the smoothed popularity prior blended into candidate scores.
MMR-lite relevance/diversity balance (percent toward relevance).
Cross-category bridge recs (ADR-027, the moat mechanic). Flag exists for rollout control only — the capability is a required deliverable.
Bridge picks injected per (source, target) category pair.

description: Onboarding staples quiz, whiskey (JSON; editable without deploy).
Onboarding staples quiz, wine BY VARIETAL (style codes; each resolves to a representative catalog drink so quiz ratings stay real ratings).
Confidence (percent, 0-100) assigned to drink attribute values inherited from style baselines (ADR-009). Vector sync reads this.
Minimum pg_trgm name similarity (percent) for a producer pair to enter the merge queue.
Minimum pg_trgm name similarity (percent) for a same-category drink pair to enter the merge queue.
